hypotheses.label: grimoire's grammar mutators place structural tokens/constructs into the corpus that naive's byte havoc cannot, building larger/deeper inputs

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: joint_necessity's default arms are vpc-resolving / cmplog-blocking / vp-blocking. For this shape repoint resolving_arm=grimoire, blocking_arm=naive (a param, no code change to the measurement). The metrics carry over: tag_lift = grimoire_tags vs naive_tags log/ratio; size_lift = median grimoire seed size vs median naive seed size; naive_tags = structural-token count in the naive (blocking) seed set. The original vpc/vp/cmplog metric names are inapplicable here; the arbiter reads grimoire_tags, naive_tags, tag_lift, size_lift.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + magic ('true','ttcf') the GeneralizationStage preserves (see harfbuzz_5440_h2 'true'/'loca'/'ttcf', harfbuzz_6181 multi-table). libxml2 tokens are XML/DTD structural constructs grimoire splices that naive byte havoc does not (see libxml2_15063 repeated ATTLIST, libxml2_6924 namespace decls, libxml2_9784 multi-construct DOCTYPE skeleton). The per-target token vocab is a measurement param; the descriptor is target-agnostic.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_tags ~ 0 (byte havoc places essentially no coherent structural tokens) AND grimoire_tags >> naive_tags (grammar mutators assemble the constructs) AND size_lift >= 1.0 (grimoire seeds are at least as large/deep, often larger). A pure-byte-mutation alternative (no grammar awareness) would predict naive_tags ~ grimoire_tags and tag_lift ~ 0, so a positive tag_lift discriminates grammar-aware assembly from byte havoc.
hypotheses.rule: tag_lift >= 1.0 AND naive_tags < grimoire_tags AND grimoire_tags >= 2
hypotheses.rule_explanation: tag_lift >= 1.0 means grimoire carries >= ~2x the structural tokens of naive; the absolute floor grimoire_tags >= 2 guards against both arms being near-zero (which would be non-discriminating). naive_tags < grimoire_tags enforces the directional gap.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: if naive's havoc-derived corpus happened to carry as many table tags / XML constructs as grimoire's, tag_lift -> 0 and the hypothesis is refuted. Rules OUT the alternative 'naive blocks for an unrelated reason (coverage scheduling, energy) while both corpora carry equal structure'.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape: the shape was labelled grimoire-pro from WHICH fuzzer resolved; this test measures the STRUCTURAL CONTENT of the seed corpora (token count + size), never re-reads the resolve/block outcome. Token presence is a property of the bytes, orthogonal to whether a branch flipped.

hypotheses.label: fallback sub-hypothesis: grimoire reaches the gate via larger/deeper structure without a target-vocabulary token signal (size lift but flat token count)

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: same repoint; uses only the size_lift metric (subset of joint_necessity's outputs), for branches whose construct is a topology/preservation property not captured by a flat token list.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 (grimoire seeds are materially larger/deeper) even when tag_lift is ~0, because the gate is about element-tree DEPTH or a preserved sub-token rather than a countable table tag. A byte-havoc-only alternative would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 (no systematic size advantage).
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Only consulted by the arbiter when structural_token_assembly does NOT fire (its token signal is null/flat) — see decision_order. Catches depth/preservation branches the token list misses.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If grimoire and naive seeds are the same size, size_lift -> 1.0 and this is refuted.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Size is a byte-length property of the corpus, independent of the resolve label.

respected: on-disk corpora + DB seed sets exist ONLY for the relevant arms on harfbuzz; libxml2 has NO usable seed sets (see measurability below). The descriptor scores only branches whose grimoire-resolving AND naive seed sets are present.
no seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence descriptor is used (I2S/grimoire-stage events are untagged in mutation_op beyond the Grimoire* mutator names, and we do not rely on counting those).

byte_freq_ratio is NOT used: grimoire-vs-naive winning/blocking seeds are different-SIZED structural-assembly inputs, so fixed-offset byte alignment fails (data_reality 3). Token-count + size_ratio are used instead, exactly as data_reality 3 prescribes.

harfbuzz.evidence: DB carries grimoire-resolving seeds for all 6 and naive-resolving seeds for 5311/5810/5866/5958 (naive is captured for these even though Layer-1 labels the shape grimoire-pro); naive seed set + on-disk corpus available. joint_necessity (repointed to grimoire vs naive) can score these. 5440 has grimoire-resolving but the contrast arm is cmplog/vp (naive absent) — see caveats.

libxml2.reason: libxml2 is NOT in the on-disk-corpus target set (data_reality 4: only curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3), AND 35/36 of these branches have ZERO resolving_seeds and ZERO blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (verified). The lone exception, libxml2_6210, carries only value_profile-resolving / value_profile_cmplog-blocking seeds — neither the grimoire-resolving nor the naive arm this test needs. No corpus and no seeds => no struct_size_and_token_count input. These 36 branches fall to fallback: inconclusive by data limitation, NOT by failed test.

notes: MERGES: all 8 prior clusters collapse to ONE discriminable hypothesis (structural_token_assembly) plus one fallback sub-hypothesis (structural_depth_only). The 8 prior labels (namespace_declaration_assembly / degenerate_field_collapse / multi_construct_co_occurrence / dtd_declaration_multi_construct / font_multi_table_assembly / element_tree_topology / token_preservation / quantitative_accumulation) all describe the SAME underlying technique-effect: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural splice/recurse mutators place coherent grammar tokens/constructs (XML declarations, DTD bodies, font table tags, namespace decls) that naive byte havoc cannot — only the SPECIFIC construct and the gate it feeds differ per cluster. No real-data measurement separates 'assembles a namespace decl' from 'assembles an ATTLIST' from 'assembles a multi-table font': all reduce to 'grimoire corpus carries structural tokens that naive's lacks' (tag_lift > 0, size_lift >= 1) — exactly the joint_necessity token_count machinery, repointed from the vpc/cmplog arms to grimoire/naive. Keeping 8 fuzzy clusters would be 8 copies of one rule; collapsing to 1 sharp token-count rule is the discriminable set. structural_depth_only is kept SEPARATE only because token_preservation + element_tree_topology branches encode the construct as DEPTH/a-preserved-subtoken rather than a countable target-vocabulary token, so they need size_lift even when tag_lift is flat — that IS a real-data-separable distinction (size vs token-vocab). PER-TARGET TOKEN VOCAB: harfbuzz = 4-byte sfnt table tags + 'true'/'ttcf' magic (the GeneralizationStage preserves them, per harfbuzz_5440_h2 / 6181); libxml2 = XML/DTD structural constructs (<?xml, <!DOCTYPE, <!ATTLIST, xmlns, etc.). The vocab is a measurement param, NOT a new descriptor. TARGETS: only harfbuzz (6 branches) is measurable; libxml2 (36 branches) is NOT — no on-disk corpus AND no DB seed sets, so 36/42 branches carry to fallback: inconclusive as a HONEST data limitation, not a refutation. CAVEATS: (1) the dominant data reality is that this shape is 86% libxml2 and libxml2 is unmeasurable on the seed/corpus axis — the evidence test can only confirm/refute the grimoire-structural mechanism on the 6 harfbuzz branches and must transfer the conclusion to libxml2 by analogy, which is weaker; a future descriptor sourced from source_cfg or from re-running per_role_coverage seeds (db/per_role_coverage exists for libxml2 W/L sets) could recover the libxml2 branches if a struct_size_and_token_count over those on-disk W/L dumps were built (registry_tool: null candidate, NOT proposed here since it requires a new source). (2) harfbuzz_5440_h2's contrast arm is cmplog/value_profile (naive seed set absent), so its naive_tags is undefined; if naive seeds cannot be obtained the arbiter should treat 5440 as inconclusive rather than force a grimoire-vs-cmplog contrast (cmplog already carries I2S and would muddy the grammar-vs-byte-havoc discrimination). (3) the shape's Layer-1 label is grimoire-resolves/naive-blocks, but DB seed capture shows naive ALSO resolved several harfbuzz branches late — the token-count contrast remains valid (it measures structural content, not the resolve outcome, satisfying G2) but the gap may be smaller than the per-branch analyses (which contrast grimoire vs cmplog) imply; tag_lift >= 1.0 (~2x) is set conservatively for this reason.
